title: Express 5 Routing, Path Syntax, Parameters, and Dispatch · Contract Lens: Params Decoding Validation
summary: An Express 4 route table is moved unchanged, wildcard values change shape, optional syntax breaks, route order shadows literals, and decoded parameters are trusted as identifiers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts params decoding validation increment in Express 5 route grammar and dispatch table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat decoded string or wildcard-array parameters as untrusted input and validate identifier grammar, length, canonical form, and existence separately.
Derive dispatch from registration order, method, path, router mounting, next-route behavior, and literal-versus-parameter overlap.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Params Decoding Validation · Theory Params Decoding Validation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3643 in a document service receiving slow compressed uploads. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to preserve Express 5 route order and named wildcard-array evidence; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
